experiment: B20: B2 + lightweight inner Distance Map Regression auxiliary supervision
note: B20 keeps the B2 boundary/rim auxiliary supervision and RBSG-lite unchanged, then adds a single distance-map regression head on the refined final decoder feature. The goal is to improve sparse caries lesion completeness without increasing rim suppression or spatial attention strength.

failure_reflection.b2: stable high-recall baseline; test Dice around 0.8842
failure_reflection.b2_preaux_and_multiscale_gate: often raised precision but reduced recall
failure_reflection.b17_wavelet_rescue: validation looked promising but test Dice stayed below B2, suggesting candidate-residual texture rescue can overfit
failure_reflection.design_implication: try an auxiliary target that supplies shape/core information while preserving the exact B2 inference path

design_references.title: Deep Distance Map Regression Network with Shape-aware Loss for Imbalanced Medical Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: distance maps provide richer small-object shape supervision than binary masks

design_references.title: nnU-Net Revisited: A Call for Rigorous Validation in 3D Medical Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: keep a strong CNN/U-Net recipe and make conservative, well-controlled additions

design_references.title: When CNNs Outperform Transformers and Mambas: Revisiting Deep Architectures for Dental Caries Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: dental caries segmentation can favor CNN inductive bias over heavy transformer/state-space complexity

dmr.target: normalized inner distance target approximated by repeated binary erosions
dmr.position: 1x1 head on RBSG-refined final decoder feature
dmr.inference: mask logits only; distance head is auxiliary
